description:
You are a capacity-planner who has just finished analysing the most recent resource-usage data for the “capacity-planner” project that lives in the user’s home directory.  
 Bump the project to the next <minor> semantic version (from 1.4.2 to 1.5.0), write a standards-compliant changelog entry, and publish the numeric results of your analysis in a release-note file.

 Starting Situation
 ├─ Directory: /home/user/projects/capacity-planner
 │  ├─ VERSION                (text file, single line: 1.4.2)
 │  ├─ CHANGELOG.md           (already exists, Keep-a-Changelog format, contains entries up to 1.4.2)
 │  ├─ data/
 │  │   └─ usage.log          (CSV, “timestamp,cpu,mem” – raw data you must parse)
 │  └─ release_notes/         (may or may not exist yet)

 Your tasks, in order:

 1. Bump the version  
    • Edit /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/VERSION so the single line now reads exactly:  
      1.5.0

 2. Update the changelog  
    • Prepend a brand-new section at the very top of /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/CHANGELOG.md, immediately after the first “# Changelog” heading.  
    • The section MUST follow the “Keep a Changelog 1.0” layout and look *exactly* like the template below (unchanged spacing, headings and bullet punctuation) except that you must fill the `<date>` placeholder with the literal string 2023-09-30 (the automated test will compare the entire block verbatim):  

      ## [1.5.0] - 2023-09-30  
      ### Added  
      - Capacity analysis summary for Q3 2023 (see `release_notes/1.5.0.txt`).  

      ### Changed  
      - Updated default forecast window from 30 d to 90 d in `planner.py`.  

    • Do **not** remove any older entries.

 3. Publish detailed release notes  
    • Make sure the directory /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/release_notes exists (create it if necessary).  
    • Create a new file /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/release_notes/1.5.0.txt with the following **exact** seven-line, newline-terminated format; replace every `<value>` placeholder with the numbers you calculate from data/usage.log, rounded to one decimal place:

      CAPACITY ANALYSIS SUMMARY  
      =========================  
      CPU_AVG: <value>  
      CPU_PEAK: <value>  
      MEM_AVG: <value>  
      MEM_PEAK: <value>  

      Notes:  
      • Values are percentages.  
      • Each line must end with a Unix newline (LF).  
      • The heading line of equal signs (======) must be 25 characters long exactly as shown above.

 4. How to calculate the numbers  
    • Parse every line of /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/data/usage.log (comma-separated values: timestamp,cpu,mem).  
    • CPU_AVG = arithmetic mean of the cpu column.  
    • CPU_PEAK = maximum of the cpu column.  
    • MEM_AVG = arithmetic mean of the mem column.  
    • MEM_PEAK = maximum of the mem column.  
    • Round each number to exactly one decimal place (standard rounding).

 5. Verification expectations  
    After you finish, the automated tests will check:  
    a. /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/VERSION contains only “1.5.0\n”.  
    b. The first new section in CHANGELOG.md matches the block shown in step 2 *byte-for-byte*.  
    c. /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/release_notes/1.5.0.txt exists, is exactly seven lines long, ends with a trailing newline, and the four numeric fields contain your correctly calculated, one-decimal-place values.

 End result: the project is on version 1.5.0, the changelog is updated, and the quantitative resource-usage summary is available for other engineers to consume.
truth:
Initial file hierarchy and contents before the agent starts:

 /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/
 ├─ VERSION
 │     1.4.2\n
 ├─ CHANGELOG.md
 │     # Changelog
 │     ...
 │     ## [1.4.2] - 2023-08-10
 │     ### Fixed
 │     - Patched forecasting overflow bug.
 │     (plus any older entries; tests ignore them)
 ├─ data/usage.log
 │     2023-08-01T00:00:00Z,45.2,60.1
 │     2023-08-01T01:00:00Z,55.8,72.3
 │     2023-08-01T02:00:00Z,88.1,95.4
 │     2023-08-01T03:00:00Z,36.4,50.2
 │     2023-08-01T04:00:00Z,50.0,65.0
 │     2023-08-01T05:00:00Z,70.0,80.0
 │     (each line ends with LF)
 └─ release_notes/         (empty directory already present)

 Correct numeric answers the tests will expect in release_notes/1.5.0.txt:

 CPU_AVG: 57.6
 CPU_PEAK: 88.1
 MEM_AVG: 70.5
 MEM_PEAK: 95.4

 The file /home/user/projects/capacity-planner/release_notes/1.5.0.txt must therefore contain exactly:

 Line 1: “CAPACITY ANALYSIS SUMMARY\n”
 Line 2: “=========================\n”
 Line 3: “CPU_AVG: 57.6\n”
 Line 4: “CPU_PEAK: 88.1\n”
 Line 5: “MEM_AVG: 70.5\n”
 Line 6: “MEM_PEAK: 95.4\n”
 Line 7: “\n”    (blank line, **must** be present)

 The tests will also confirm that:
 • VERSION file now reads “1.5.0\n”.
 • A new “## [1.5.0] - 2023-09-30” section appears immediately after the first “# Changelog” line and its body is byte-for-byte identical to the template given in the public <task> block.
